verses.t: Josiah kept the Pesach to his Yahuah in Yerushalayim; he killed the Pesach lamb on the fourteenth day of the first month,
having placed the kohanim according to their divisions, arrayed in their garments, in the heykal of Yahuah.

And he told the Levites, the heykal servants of Yasharal, that they should sanctify themselves to Yahuah and put the holy ark of Yahuah in the house which Shelomoh the king, the son of Dawiḏ, had built;
and he said, "You need no longer carry it upon your shoulders. Now worship Yahuah your Aluahim and serve his people Yasharal; and prepare yourselves by your families and kindred,

in accordance with the directions of Dawiḏ king of Yasharal and the magnificence of Shelomoh his son. Stand in order in the heykal according to the groupings of the fathers' houses of you Levites, who minister before your brethren the people of Yasharal,
and kill the Pesach lamb and prepare the sacrifices for your brethren, and keep the Pesach according to the mitsvah of Yahuah which was given to Mosheh."

And Josiah gave to the people who were present thirty thousand lambs and kids, and three thousand calves; these were given from the king's possessions, as he promised, to the people and the kohanim and Levites.

And Hilkiah, Zechariah, and Jehiel, the chief officers of the heykal, gave to the kohanim for the Pesach two thousand six hundred sheep and three hundred calves. [9} And Jeconiah and Shemaiah and Nethanel his brother, and Hashabiah and Ochiel and Joram, captains over thousands, gave the Levites for the Pesach five thousand sheep and seven hundred calves.
And this is what took place. The kohanim and the Levites, properly arrayed and having the unleavened bread, stood according to kindred
and the grouping of the fathers' houses, before the people, to make the offering to Yahuah as it is written in the book of Mosheh; this they did in the morning.
They roasted the Pesach lamb with fire, as required; and they boiled the sacrifices in brass pots and caldrons, with a pleasing odor,

and carried them to all the people. Afterward they prepared the Pesach for themselves and for their brethren the kohanim, the sons of Aharon, [14} because the kohanim were offering the fat until night; so the Levites prepared it for themselves and for their brethren the kohanim, the sons of Aharon.
And the heykal singers, the sons of Asaph, were in their place according to the arrangement made by Dawiḏ, and also Asaph,

And this is what took place. The kohanim and the Levites, properly arrayed and having the unleavened bread, stood according to kindred
and the grouping of the fathers' houses, before the people, to make the offering to Yahuah as it is written in the book of Mosheh; this they did in the morning.
They roasted the Pesach lamb with fire, as required; and they boiled the sacrifices in brass pots and caldrons, with a pleasing odor,

and carried them to all the people. Afterward they prepared the Pesach for themselves and for their brethren the kohanim, the sons of Aharon, [14} because the kohanim were offering the fat until night; so the Levites prepared it for themselves and for their brethren the kohanim, the sons of Aharon.
And the heykal singers, the sons of Asaph, were in their place according to the arrangement made by Dawiḏ, and also Asaph, Zechariah, and Eddinus, who represented the king.
The gatekeepers were at each gate; no one needed to depart from his duties, for their brethren the Levites prepared the Pesach for them.
So the things that had to do with the sacrifices to Yahuah were accomplished that day: the Pesach was kept
and the sacrifices were offered on the mizbe'ach of Yahuah, according to the command of King Josiah.
And the people of Yasharal who were present at that time kept the Pesach and the feast of unleavened bread seven days.
No Pesach like it had been kept in Yasharal since the times of Shemu'al the naḇi;
none of the kings of Yasharal had kept such a Pesach as was kept by Josiah and the kohanim and Levites and the men of Yahuḏah and all of Yasharal who were dwelling in Yerushalayim.
In the eighteenth year of the reign of Josiah this Pesach was kept
And the deeds of Josiah were upright in the sight of Yahuah, for his heart was full of godliness.

The events of his reign have been recorded in the past, concerning those who sinned and acted wickedly toward Yahuah beyond any other people or kingdom, and how they grieved Yahuah deeply, so that the words of Yahuah rose up against Yasharal.
After all these acts of Josiah, it happened that Pharaoh, king of Mitsrayim, went to make war at Carchemish on the Euphrates, and Josiah went out against him.
And the king of Mitsrayim sent word to him saying, "What have we to do with each other, king of Judea?
I was not sent against you by Yahuah Aluahim, for my war is at the Euphrates. And now Yahuah is with me! Yahuah is with me, urging me on! Stand aside, and do not oppose Yahuah."
But Josiah did not turn back to his chariot, but tried to fight with him, and did not heed the words of Yirmeyahu the naḇi from the mouth of Yahuah.
He joined battle with him in the plain of Megiddo, and the commanders came down against King Josiah.
And the king said to his servants, "Take me away from the battIe, for I am very weak." And immediately his servants took him out of the line of battIe.
And he got into his second chariot; and after he was brought back to Yerushalayim he died, and was buried in the tomb of his fathers.

And in all Judea they mourned for Josiah. Yirmeyahu the naḇi lamented for Josiah, and the principal men, with the women, have made lamentation for him to this day; it was ordained that this should always be done throughout the whole nation of Yasharal.

These things are written in the book of the histories of the kings of Judea; and every one of the acts of Josiah, and his splendor, and his understanding of the law of Yahuah, and the things that he had done before and these that are now told, are recorded in the book of the kings of Yasharal and Yahuḏah.
And the men of the nation took Jeconiah the son of Josiah, who was twenty­ three years old, and made him king in succession to Josiah his father.
And he reigned three months in Yahuḏah and Yerushalayim. Then the king of Mitsrayim deposed him from reigning in Yerushalayim,
and fined the nation a hundred talents of silver and a talent of gold.
And the king of Mitsrayim made Jehoiakim his brother king of Judea and Yerushalayim.
Jehoiakim put the nobles in prison, and seized his brother Zarius and brought him up out of Mitsrayim.
Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he began to reign in Judea and Yerushalayim, and he did what was evil in the sight of Yahuah.
And Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up against him, and bound him with a chain of brass and took him away to Babylon.
Nebuchadnezzar also took some holy vessels of Yahuah, and carried them away, and stored them in his heykal in Babylon.
But the things that are reported about Jehoiakim and his uncleanness and impiety are written in the chronicles of the kings.
Jehoiachin his son became king in his stead; when he was made king he was eighteen years old,
and he reigned three months and ten days in Yerushalayim. He did what was evil in the sight of Yahuah.
So after a year Nebuchadnezzar sent and removed him to Babylon, with the holy vessels of Yahuah,
and made Zedekiah king of Judea and Yerushalayim. Zedekiah was twenty-one years old, and he reigned Aleven years.
He also did what was evil in the sight of Yahuah, and did not heed the words that were spoken by Yirmeyahu the naḇi from the mouth of Yahuah.

And though King Nebuchadnezzar had made him swear by the name of Yahuah, he broke his oath and rebelled; and he stiffened his neck and hardened his heart and transgressed the laws of Yahuah, the Aluahim of Yasharal.

Even the leaders of the people and of the kohanim committed many acts of sacrilege and lawlessness beyond all the unclean deeds of all the nations, and polluted the heykal of Yahuah which had been hallowed in Yerushalayim.
So the Aluahim of their fathers sent by his mal'ak to call them back, because he would have spared them and his dwelling place.
But they mocked his mal'akim, and whenever Yahuah spoke, they scoffed at his neḇi'im,
until in his anger against his people because of their ungodly acts he gave command to bring against them the kings of the Chaldeans.
These slew their young men with the sword around their holy heykal, and did not spare young man or virgin, old man or child, for he gave them all into their hands.
And all the holy vessels of Yahuah, great and small, and the treasure chests of Yahuah, and the royal stores, they took and carried away to Babylon.
And they burned the house of Yahuah and broke down the walls of Yerushalayim and burned their towers with fire,
and utterly destroyed all its glorious things. The survivors he led away to Babylon with the sword,
and they were servants to him and to his sons until the Persians began to reign, in fulfillment of the word of Yahuah by the mouth of Yirmeyahu:
"Until the land has enjoyed its Shabbaths, it shall keep Shabbath all the time of its desolation until the completion of seventy years."
"From thee is the victory; from thee is wisdom, and your is the glory. I am thy servant.
Baruk art thou, who hast given me wisdom; I give thee thanks, 0 Yahuah of our fathers."
So he took the letters, and went to Babylon and told this to all his brethren.
And they praised the Aluahim of their fathers, because he had given them release and permission
to go up and build Yerushalayim and the heykal which is called by his name; and they feasted, with music and rejoicing, for seven days.
while many came with trumpets and a joyful noise,
so that the people could not hear the trumpets because of the weeping of the people. For the multitude sounded the trumpets loudly, so that the sound was heard afar;
and when the enemies of the tribe of Yahuḏah and Binyamin heard it, they came to find out what the sound of the trumpets meant.
And they learned that those who had returned from captivity were building the heykal for Yahuah Aluahim of Yasharal.
So they approached Zerubbabel and Jeshua and the heads of the fathers' houses and said to them, "We will build with you.
For we obey your Yahuah just as you do and we have been sacrificing to him ever since the days of Esarhaddon king of the Assyrians, who brought us here."
But Zerubbabel and Jeshua and the heads of the fathers' houses in Yasharal said to them, "You have nothing to do with us in building the house for Yahuah our Aluahim,
for we alone will build it for Yahuah of Yasharal, as Cyrus the king of the Persians has commanded us."
But the peoples of the land pressed hard upon those in Judea, cut off Yoseph 8. Lumpkin their supplies, and hindered their building;
and by plots and demagoguery and uprisings they prevented the completion of the building as long as King Cyrus lived. And they were kept from building for two years, until the reign of Darius.
